title: Electrical and electronics technicians
description: Apply electrical and electronic theory and related knowledge, usually under the direction of engineering staff, to design, build, repair, adjust, and modify electrical components, circuitry, controls, and machinery for subsequent evaluation and use by engineering staff in making engineering design decisions. Operate, test, maintain, or adjust unmanned, automated, servomechanical, or electromechanical equipment. May operate unmanned submarines, aircraft, or other equipment to observe or record visual information at sites such as oil rigs, crop fields, buildings, or for similar infrastructure, deep ocean exploration, or hazardous waste removal. May assist engineers in testing and designing robotics equipment. Build, install, test, or maintain robotic equipment or related automated production systems. (Based on 17 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Observe sets or locations for potential problems and determine filming and lighting requirements.
Confer with crew members to discuss assignments and determine filming sequences, desired effects, camera movements, and lighting requirements.
Use various camera mounts and operate zoom lenses to change images according to specifications and instructions.
Operate cameras and broadcast equipment to record scenes for television, advertising, or motion pictures, adjusting controls as needed.
Design and modify equipment to employer specifications while collaborating with engineers and technicians.
Report equipment problems, perform repairs, and ensure that maintenance tasks are completed effectively and timely.
Install, adjust, and operate electronic equipment to record, edit, and transmit various media formats.
Test, clean, maintain, and repair electronic equipment to ensure proper working conditions for broadcasts and installations.
Assemble electrical systems or prototypes according to engineering instructions, applying knowledge of electronic components.
Diagnose and troubleshoot equipment and systems to resolve performance issues using technical instruments.
Maintain inventories of spare parts and equipment, requisitioning as necessary to support operations.
Prepare and maintain records detailing tests, repairs, and maintenance to document equipment performance.
Check signal quality and connectivity of cables, recording results for quality assurance in broadcasting.
Inspect and test electrical or electronic systems to locate and diagnose malfunctions using testing instruments such as oscilloscopes and voltmeters.
Install equipment and accessories such as communication equipment, navigation equipment, and security systems.
Confer with customers to determine the nature of malfunctions and advise them concerning equipment operation or maintenance.
Record results of diagnostic tests and maintain records of equipment maintenance work or repairs.
Repair, adjust, or replace electrical, mechanical, or electronic components using hand tools and soldering equipment.
Test performance of components or circuits of faulty equipment using test equipment or software to diagnose malfunctions.
Operate machines to test functioning of parts or mechanisms and demonstrate proper use of equipment.
Maintain parts inventories and order any additional parts needed for repairs or assembly.
Build, install, or maintain robotic equipment or related automated production systems according to specifications.
Train new repairers and customers or other personnel to install, use, or maintain robotic or electronic systems.
Investigate accidents or injuries and prepare reports of findings.
Meet with vendors or suppliers to discuss products used in repair work.
Confer with personnel, such as management, engineering, or quality control, to coordinate work activities and identify resource needs.
Recommend or initiate personnel actions, such as hires, promotions, or disciplinary measures.
Perform skilled repair or maintenance operations, using tools such as hydraulic presses, shears, or welding equipment.
Compile operational or personnel records, such as production records or maintenance statistics.
Develop, implement, or evaluate maintenance policies and procedures.
Inspect, test, and measure completed work using hand tools or gauges to verify conformance to standards.
Develop or implement electronic maintenance programs or information management systems.

emerging_tasks.task: Operate drones for aerial videography and photography during live events or for pre-recorded material.
Operate drones to capture aerial or unique angle footage for film, television, or video productions.
Perform installation, testing, adjustment, and repair of avionics equipment in uncrewed aerial vehicles, such as drones.
Use drones for inspection of high-voltage lines and other hard-to-reach equipment.
Program and calibrate drones for specific missions or tasks, ensuring proper functionality and performance.
